title: Clank & Slop - The Tape
tagline: - READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE -

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The tape is frozen and the news is not. SK Hynix's US debut closed near $170, up about 13% on its $149 offer for a raise above $26bn, but with US futures shut until Sunday evening every other print here is Friday's close while the Iran strikes run live and unpriced.

Oil is carrying a partial war premium. Brent settled at $76.01 and WTI at $71.41, below $80 despite a third US strike round, as the market bets naval escort and talks prevent a sustained Hormuz outage; the claimable transit data is the IMO's lagged averages, not paid ship-tracking counts.

The coding agents are fighting on price. Anthropic's hard hourly and weekly caps meet OpenAI's pooled credits as a Claude Code limit boost nears its 13 July expiry; benchmarks still make headlines, but the customers who spend the most now choose their agent on the meter.

props.title: The Tape
props.kicker: Fri close · stale to Sun eve

items.why: CME's S&P, Treasury and WTI futures reopen Sunday evening ET; the first live prices have to absorb a third round of US strikes the Friday tape never saw.
Anthropic's temporary weekly-limit boost lapses and the strong Fable tier moves toward metered credits; watch whether the caps are softened or the 'claudex' switch accelerates.

The when-issued line converts to the permanent SKHY ticker and GraniteShares' leveraged SKUU and SKDD funds are expected; the 13% debut gain gets its first regular-way test. Launch date still needs desk confirmation.
France meet Spain in Dallas, then Argentina meet England in Atlanta; the second is the most politically loaded fixture the game can produce.
The Treasury's revoked licence requires all previously authorised Iran oil business to conclude; the sanctions clock and the strike rounds are one instrument now.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-12 · Foreman, Sprockett, Tinkerton, Cogsworth, Graves · one call settled a miss (the Argentina-England semi did come to pass)

open_calls.question: A sitting minister in Buenos Aires or London publicly ties the Argentina-England semi-final to the Malvinas before kickoff
open_calls.detail: The chant belongs to the terraces and the dressing room; so far the state has stayed out, and Scaloni called it 'just a football game'. A politicisation needs someone with power to choose it, against the incentive to keep the match sporting.

open_calls.question: Anthropic publicly loosens or extends Claude Code weekly limits after the 13 July boost expiry
open_calls.detail: The direction of travel is toward metered credits, not looser caps; the likelier response to the backlash is paid overage and a nudge to higher tiers rather than a public climbdown, though a capacity scare could force a temporary reprieve.

open_calls.question: Credible reporting confirms an RSF ground assault on El Obeid, beyond the current siege and drone campaign
open_calls.detail: The siege warning still holds with no verified fresh assault in the latest window; the El Fasher precedent keeps the base rate high, offset by the RSF's incentive to avoid a second genocide finding and any negotiated pause.

open_calls.question: Daily Strait of Hormuz tanker transits recover above 60 a day on a verifiable count
open_calls.detail: A third strike round and an Iranian control claim push the odds of a clean recovery lower; the claimable IMO averages sit well below 60, and a move above needs a stretch with no fresh incident.

open_calls.question: France or Syria announces an additional security or reconstruction mechanism tied to Macron's Damascus visit
open_calls.detail: The visit carried reconstruction language, but the probability stays below even until an official mechanism, not a statement, appears; Macron's week is turned toward Monday's Paris coalition meeting on Ukraine.

items.what: SK Hynix's US depositary shares closed their debut near $170, about 13% above the $149 offer, for a raise above $26bn; the PHLX Semiconductor Index barely moved, and Micron lifted planned US investment past $250bn through 2035.

Brent settled at $76.01 and WTI at $71.41, lower on the day but higher on the week, holding below $80 despite a third US strike round; claimable Hormuz transit data is the IMO's lagged averages, not real-time counts.

The yen firmed to around 161.7 per dollar after Japan's finance minister pushed pension money toward domestic assets, and Tokyo moved to write central-bank independence into its economic blueprint to calm a jittery bond market.
